programme.title: NHK World English News Morning
programme.desc: English news from Japan's Broadcasting Corporation, NHK, delivering the latest in business, technology, health, environmental and world news.

programme.title: euronews
programme.desc: Headlines, Breaking News & Analysis from the Euronews news centre in Brussels, Belgium, in English.

programme.title: ANC Philippines The World Tonight
programme.desc: Delivers the top stories of the day, covering political, economic, and social sectors on both local and global fronts.

programme.title: DW English News
programme.desc: News and analysis of the top international and European news and current affairs from Berlin, in English.

programme.title: Al Jazeera News
programme.desc: News via satellite from Al Jazeera Satellite Network, Qatar, in English.

programme.title: PBS News Horizons
programme.desc: Dive into the world of science, health, technology and environmental issues making headlines each week, hosted by PBS News Hour's William Brangham, with in-depth discussions featuring leading experts and professionals.

programme.title: France 24 English News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: DD India News Hour
programme.desc: The most recent political, sports, entertainment and socio-economic updates across India and the world from New Delhi, in English.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw Bitesize
programme.desc: Join host Adam Liaw and all your favourite chefs, cooks and celebrities for the best bits from The Cook Up kitchen.

programme.title: The Bee Whisperer
programme.desc: We're heating up it in this episode! Charlie and friends look to brave the 100F degrees Texas heat to remove beehives from an old car and an RV. They face several challenges, including cutting through bamboo and avoiding the risk of impaling themselves on punji sticks. Even the camera crew begin to feel the heat.

programme.title: Wales: Land of the Wild
programme.desc: It's the first day of spring but winter has a sting in its tail as the country is blanketed with snow. As the sun returns the breeding season finally gets underway.

programme.title: South Africa from Above
programme.desc: From the West to Eastern Cape, embark from Cape Town to travel along the spectacular Garden Route. Home to thousands of plant species found nowhere else on earth, this rich landscape and coastline supports a diverse mix of mammals, birds and sea creatures.

programme.title: PBS News Compass Points
programme.desc: Join PBS News Hour's Nick Schifrin and a panel of experts each week analysing prominent international affairs topics and America's role in a shifting world, breaking down global conflicts, rising tensions, and their implications for U.S. interests.

programme.title: PBS Washington Week with the Atlantic
programme.desc: PBS Washington Week with The Atlantic is the longest-running primetime news and public affairs program on television. The show features a group of journalists participating in a weekly roundtable discussion of major news events. Moderated by Jeffrey Goldberg, Editor-in-Chief of The Atlantic.

programme.title: Point: Road To Referendum History Bites
programme.desc: We reflect on the legacy of the Aboriginal Tent Embassy in Canberra, set up by four young men in a protest outside Parliament House.

programme.title: ProMX Championship
programme.desc: The 2026 ProMX Championship heads to the nation's capital for round two at the technically challenging Canberra Motocross Track.

programme.title: Paris-Roubaix Highlights
programme.desc: All the best moments from the 2026 Paris-Roubaix Men's race, as the peloton takes on a 258.3 km route through France, starting in Compiegne and finishing in the Roubaix velodrome.

programme.title: Paris-Roubaix Highlights
programme.desc: All the best moments from the 2026 Paris-Roubaix Femmes, as the peloton takes on a 148.5 km route through France, starting in Denain and finishing in the Roubaix velodrome.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: K'gari, also known as Fraser Island, is Ernie's next destination. He meets up with a proud Butchulla man, a four-wheel drive enthusiast, and a determined woman with a special connection to whales.

programme.title: Surviving WW2
programme.desc: During WW2, 35 million soldiers were captured, five million never returning home. Four British POWs share their survival stories. Eric Taylor and Norman Lewis endured German Stalags after Dunkirk, surviving death marches to make it home. Alistair Urquhart and Harold Atcherley were captured in Singapore in 1942, forced to build Japan's notorious Death Railway, where 12,000 Allied POWs perished.

programme.title: SBS World News
programme.desc: The latest reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: The Rise and Fall of the Maya
programme.desc: In 900 AD, the Mayans left their cities in a hurry. In southern Mexico and Central America, archaeological investigation on the Mayans explores the last moments of this civilisation and the abandonment of its cities, to understand what events led to the fall of Maya civilisation. What were the reasons for such a rapid collapse: a long, intense drought, or internal wars?

programme.title: The Rise and Fall of the Maya
programme.desc: In the Mexican state of Yucatan, archaeologists are uncovering new clues to the rebirth of Mayan civilisation around the year 1000 and to the events that finally forced them to abandon their cities for good. Scientists and experts from around the world are solving the last mysteries about the disappearance of these mythical cities and proving that the last Mayans showed great resilience.

programme.title: Emperor: The Rise & Fall of a Dynasty
programme.desc: Livia, the first empress of Rome, comes to regret the scheming that put her son Tiberius on the throne, as he and his notorious heir Caligula drag the empire into an age of tyranny, incest, and shocking depravity.

programme.title: Lost Treasures of Egypt
programme.desc: Archaeologists hunt for clues to the dramatic downfall of Egypt's legendary last pharaoh - Cleopatra. They unravel the true story behind Cleopatra's famous relationships with Julius Caesar and Marc Anthony. They discover a 2000-year-old painting in the heart of the desert dating from her reign. And they hunt for her lost tomb in a secret tunnel beneath an ancient temple near Alexandria.

programme.title: Putin vs the West
programme.desc: This first episode tells the story of how, when Putin first attacked Ukraine in 2014, Europe's leaders were bitterly divided over how to stop him. After massive protests erupt in Kyiv and Ukraine's president Victor Yanukovych flees to Russia, Putin makes the most audacious move of his presidency to date - sending troops into Crimea. We go behind the scenes for the critical summits and fraught phone calls that follow, as the West tries to find some way to push back - particularly after the fighting spreads to Donbas and Malaysian Airlines flight MH17 is shot down.

programme.title: Putin vs the West
programme.desc: Buoyed by his success in Crimea, Putin turns to the Middle East. Here, Western leaders try to enlist the Russian President as peacemaker - but Putin has other ideas, showing just how far he is willing to go to keep his allies in power. He can't stop the overthrow of Gaddafi in Libya, but in Syria, Putin intervenes with brutal force to prop-up Bashar al-Assad. British Prime Minister David Cameron describes his attempts to win Putin round for a peace initiative in Syria - wooing the judo-loving Russian President at the London Olympics and sharing a helicopter ride above the Winter Olympics site at Sochi.

programme.title: Putin vs the West
programme.desc: This episode takes us right up to the moment Putin launches his invasion of Ukraine. From an audacious poisoning in the British city of Salisbury to clashes with Donald Trump over nuclear missiles and fiery rhetoric aimed at NATO, the Russian President ramps up his aggression towards the West. And when Putin sets his sights on taking Ukraine, Western leaders reveal their secret meetings and crisis summits as they try to pull Putin back from the brink.

programme.title: Matched
programme.sub-title: The Muslim Wedding Of Rehana And Jansher / Ravinder And Manpreet's Union Of Two Souls
programme.desc: Muslim couple Rehana and Jansher have booked a date, but it could all come grinding to a halt if Jansher's mother doesn't give her approval when she meets the bride for the first time just days out from the wedding. And Indian bride Manpreet faces a wedding in New Zealand without the involvement of her loving parents.

programme.title: Mastermind Australia
programme.desc: Mastermind is the legendary quiz that sets the toughest questions on television. Join Mastermind quizmaster Marc Fennell, as each night he puts four contenders to the test. In this round, the specialty subjects are The Life And Career Of Tina Fey, King Gizzard And The Lizard Wizard, NASA's Apollo Program, and Attack on Titan: Season 1.

programme.title: Mastermind Australia
programme.desc: The legendary quiz that sets the toughest questions on television. Join quizmaster Marc Fennell as each night he puts four contenders to the test.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Continuing our exploration of Tasmania's Northwest, we journey further through the Country of the Parperloihener, Pennemuker, Peerapper, Manegin and Tarkiner peoples. Through dense forests and along windswept coastlines, we witness how memory, spirit, and Country remain deeply intertwined in one of Tasmania's most striking regions.

programme.title: Destination Flavour China Bitesize
programme.desc: All the best moments and bits from Destination Flavour China, led by famed chef and author Adam Liaw.

programme.title: NHK World English News Morning
programme.desc: English news from Japan's Broadcasting Corporation, NHK, delivering the latest in business, technology, health, environmental and world news.

programme.title: euronews
programme.desc: Headlines, Breaking News & Analysis from the Euronews news centre in Brussels, Belgium, in English.

programme.title: PBS News Horizons
programme.desc: Dive into the world of science, health, technology and environmental issues making headlines each week, hosted by PBS News Hour's William Brangham, with in-depth discussions featuring leading experts and professionals.

programme.title: DW English News
programme.desc: News and analysis of the top international and European news and current affairs from Berlin, in English.

programme.title: Al Jazeera News
programme.desc: News via satellite from Al Jazeera Satellite Network, Qatar, in English.

programme.title: PBS Washington Week with the Atlantic
programme.desc: PBS Washington Week with The Atlantic is the longest-running primetime news and public affairs program on television. The show features a group of journalists participating in a weekly roundtable discussion of major news events. Moderated by Jeffrey Goldberg, Editor-in-Chief of The Atlantic.

programme.title: Point: Road To Referendum History Bites
programme.desc: We reflect on the legacy of the Aboriginal Tent Embassy in Canberra, set up by four young men in a protest outside Parliament House.

programme.title: France 24 English News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: DD India News Hour
programme.desc: The most recent political, sports, entertainment and socio-economic updates across India and the world from New Delhi, in English.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw Bitesize
programme.desc: Join host Adam Liaw and all your favourite chefs, cooks and celebrities for the best bits from The Cook Up kitchen.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw Bitesize
programme.desc: Join host Adam Liaw and all your favourite chefs, cooks and celebrities for the best bits from The Cook Up kitchen.

programme.title: Matched
programme.desc: It took just one picture for Karamjeet to agree to marry Jasdeep and after an engagement conducted from afar, will love blossom for this Kiwi Indian? As life after arranged marriage is explored - did their parents get it right?

programme.title: Kew Gardens: Season by Season
programme.desc: It's winter in Kew Gardens, a carpet of snowdrops brings in the visitors, whilst in the Rock Garden Faye tells us about the world of Galanthophiles - the official name for snowdrop obsessives. Whilst down in Wakehurst Francis is tending the beautiful Winter Garden, his recent addition to the wild botanic gardens.

programme.title: Scottish Vets Down Under
programme.desc: Best friends and fellow Scottish vets Dr Chris and Dr Mike have swapped Scotland for the rural Australian town of Bendigo, where they run their own practices. In this first episode, Dr Chris takes on a puzzling case involving one of Australia's most iconic birds, whilst Dr Clare tends to a senior dog in discomfort. At the equine hospital, Dr Mike faces a tense operation on an injured newborn foal whose future hangs in the balance.

programme.title: Scottish Vets Down Under
programme.desc: Springtime in Bendigo brings out some of Australia's deadliest creatures, and the animal hospital bears the brunt of it. A succession of snake-related emergencies tests the team to their limits, with beloved family pets fighting for their lives. At the equine hospital, Dr Mike works around the clock for the season's new arrivals. Away from the surgery, Dr Chris finds that even his downtime has an unexpected sting in the tail.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 International News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: PBS News Compass Points
programme.desc: Join PBS News Hour's Nick Schifrin and a panel of experts each week analysing prominent international affairs topics and America's role in a shifting world, breaking down global conflicts, rising tensions, and their implications for U.S. interests.

programme.title: Al Jazeera News Hour
programme.desc: In-depth global news from Al Jazeera Satellite Network, Doha, Qatar, in English.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots
programme.sub-title: To The Manor Born: Glenn Close, John Waters
programme.desc: Henry Louis Gates, Jr. uncovers the lineages of actor Glenn Close and director John Waters, digging into centuries past to encounter a succession of bold forebears who found fortune amidst drama and rebellion. From the gold rush fields of the Yukon to an enclave in Quaker Pennsylvania to British royalty, our guests meet a succession of relatives who prove to be as eccentric as they are.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: Mark walks the pure white sands of WAs Esperance region, Rae cooks up a storm in Shepparton with a local legend, and Ernie shoots the breeze with a life long mate in Perth.

programme.title: Plat du Tour
programme.desc: The peloton is charging towards France's most beautiful medieval city, Carcassonne. The fortress here is the second most visited place in France (besides the Eiffel Tower, bien sur!) On Guillaume's menu is Le Carcassonnais Brioche, a dessert made with raisins and filled with deluxe creme patissiere. It's fitting tribute to the rich history of its namesake city where traditions stand as strong as its ancient stone walls.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw
programme.desc: It's a spicy sausage spectacular, and the heat is on Adam's guests, chef Ruben Lopez Mesa and radio host Megan Burslem.

programme.title: Tutankhamun: Secrets of the Toxic Tomb
programme.desc: In 1922, the tomb of Egyptian boy king Tutankhamun was unearthed. Soon, tales spread of a deadly jinx. Now, Ella Al-Shamahi reveals the extraordinary scientific truth behind the 'Pharaoh's Curse'.

programme.title: Jeopardy!
programme.desc: Ken Jennings hosts Jeopardy! Where the answers are revealed, and the contestants must guess the appropriate questions.

programme.title: Letters and Numbers
programme.desc: Two contestants pit their linguistic and numerical skills against each other and the clock in this entertaining quiz show. Join host Richard Morecroft along with maths whiz Lily Serna and wordsmith David Astle.

programme.title: Mastermind Australia
programme.desc: Tonight, The Rush Hour Trilogy, Essendon Football Club, Katniss Evergreen from The Hunger Games and professional wrestler The Undertaker. Quite the mix. Contenders: Mark Summers, Neve D'Arcy, Damon Jakovics, Kelly Wickham.

programme.title: SBS World News
programme.desc: The latest reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: Australia's Greek Cafes & Milk Bars
programme.desc: Long before global food chains arrived, Greek migrants shaped the way Australians ate, socialised and understood community. Shaken and Stirred tells the remarkable story of those who fled poverty and war to build a new life in Australia, facing prejudice and hardship along the way, yet leaving an extraordinary legacy. Drawing on rare archive footage and intimate family stories, this documentary asks what belonging really means and who gets to define it.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts a brand new episode of 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown. Jon Richardson and Big Zuu take on Danny Dyer and Helen Bauer in the classic words and numbers quiz. Mat Ewins joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers.

programme.title: Never Mind the Buzzcocks
programme.sub-title: Mel B, Alex James, Julian Clary, Sister Bliss
programme.desc: Never Mind the Buzzcocks takes on the 90's in this hour-long special. Spice Girl Mel B steps in for Daisy May Cooper as captain, joining host Greg Davies, Noel Fielding, and Jamali Maddix. Guests include Blur's Alex James, Sister Bliss from Faithless, and comedian Julian Clary, who claims he \.

programme.title: SBS World News Late
programme.desc: Reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: Herrhausen: The Banker & The Bomb
programme.desc: A successful Moscow deal establishes Herrhausen as the most influential man in global finance, as well as the very top of Red Army Faction's hit-list. Aware of the target on his back, Herrhausen is seriously injured during a simulated terrorist attack. Yet Herrhausen persists in his mission to restructure Deutsche Bank, meeting the global financial crisis head on and taking risks that others would not dream of, yet each success only escalates his vulnerability.

programme.title: Gomorrah
programme.desc: With Pietro Savastano having to leave the country and his son Gennaro's life hanging by a thread, Ciro de Marzio forges a new alliance with Don Salvatore Conte.

programme.title: Gomorrah
programme.desc: Gennaro has established a new drug trafficking network in Central America. His reunion with his father Don Pietro marks a turning point in their relationship.

programme.title: Gomorrah
programme.desc: The alliance forged by Ciro and Conte is becoming fragile. When Conte takes vengeance on one of Ciro's men, the tide turns.

programme.title: Mastermind Australia
programme.desc: The legendary quiz that sets the toughest questions on television. Join quizmaster Marc Fennell as each night he puts four contenders to the test.

programme.title: Mastermind Australia
programme.desc: The legendary quiz that sets the toughest questions on television. Join quizmaster Marc Fennell as each night he puts four contenders to the test.

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Vet Jennie saves the life of a show chicken, Darren helps a pregnant cow avoid a caesarean section, and Maurice comes face to face with a strange visitor.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: This episode of Bamay showcases beautiful Arrernte and Warlpiri Country, with locations such as Mparntwe Alice Springs and the Ellery Creek Big Hole.
